case_number: constitutional petition is disposed of as not

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No. D- 244 of 2018 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For order on MA No 1299/2018 2. For order on MA No 1300/2018 3. For katcha Peshi 4. For order on MA No 1301/2018 06.02.2018. Mr. Inam Ali Malik Advocate for the petitioner Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto Asstt:A.G Syed Meeral Shah Bukhari A.P.G a/w SIP Zahid Khoso SHO P.S.Bhan Saeedabad -=-=- Respondent No.3 has filed his comment s which are taken on record. After hearing the learned counsel for the parties at some length, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would be satisfied and shall not press this constitutional petition if petitioner is allowed to file appropriate proceedings before the trial court for his premature acquittal. Learned Asstt:A.G and learned APG have raised no objection to above proposition. In view of above this constitutional petition is disposed of as not pressed, however with direction to learned trial court that as and when the petitioner files any proceedings with regard to his premature acquittal, the trial court shall decide the same after hearing the parties within 15-days in accordance with law. In the meantime the official respondents shall strictly act in accordance with law JUDGE JUDGE
